name: generative-art
description: Creating algorithmic, generative art using p5.js with seeded randomness and interactive parameter exploration.
qualityReason: Creative use of p5.js, clear scope
capabilities: Creates algorithmic philosophy documents explaining the computational approach
Generates self-contained HTML artifacts using p5.js
Implements seeded randomness for reproducible results
Provides interactive parameter controls for exploration
Enables PNG export of generated art
author: Cam10001110101
securitySummary: This is a legitimate generative art skill with zero security risks. All 61 static findings are false positives caused by pattern matching without semantic context. The skill creates browser-based p5.js artifacts using seeded randomness for reproducible art generation. No server-side code, file system access, or command execution capabilities exist.
valueStatement: Generative art creation requires artistic vision and technical skill. This skill helps you create unique, reproducible generative art using p5.js with parameter controls for exploration. You can create flow fields, recursive patterns, particle systems, and more.
userTitle: Create generative art with seeded randomness
limitations: Runs in browser context only via generated HTML artifacts
Cannot access local files or external APIs
Depends on p5.js library loaded from CDN

useCases.title: Create unique generative pieces
useCases.description: Generate one-of-a-kind algorithmic art with full control over parameters and seeds for reproducibility.

useCases.title: Explore algorithmic aesthetics
useCases.description: Experiment with flow fields, particle systems, and recursive patterns for creative coding projects.

useCases.title: Demonstrate generative concepts
useCases.description: Show students how algorithmic processes create visual art with interactive parameter exploration.

promptTemplates.title: Simple flow field
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a flow field generative art piece with 5000 particles, subtle noise-based movement, and a warm color palette including orange and earth tones.

promptTemplates.title: Recursive structure
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate recursive branching structures inspired by natural growth patterns. Use golden ratios, varied line weights, and a cool blue color palette.

promptTemplates.title: Circle patterns
promptTemplates.prompt: Create circle packing or Voronoi tessellation generative art. Include adjustable density and relaxation parameters with earth tone colors.

promptTemplates.title: Custom philosophy
promptTemplates.prompt: Create an original generative art piece based on this concept: [describe your concept]. First write an algorithmic philosophy document, then implement it with appropriate parameters.

bestPractices: Use specific seed values to reproduce your favorite variations
Adjust one parameter at a time to understand how each affects the output
Start with conservative parameter values and gradually experiment
antiPatterns: Using excessively high particle counts may slow down the browser
Avoid very high noise scale values which can create chaotic patterns
Do not expect the same output from different seeds - each seed creates unique art

faq.question: What AI tools support this skill?
faq.answer: Works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. The skill generates HTML artifacts that run in any browser.

faq.question: Can I use my own colors?
faq.answer: Yes. The generated art includes color pickers for palette customization. You can adjust colors in real-time.

faq.question: How do I save my favorite variations?
faq.answer: Use the Download PNG button to save the current canvas. For reproducibility, note the seed number.

faq.question: Is my data safe with this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. All code runs locally in your browser. No data is sent to external servers except loading p5.js from CDN.

faq.question: Why does changing the seed create different art?
faq.answer: The skill uses seeded randomness. The same seed produces identical output. Different seeds create mathematically different but equally valid generative art.

faq.question: How is this different from static image generation?
faq.answer: Generative art creates unique algorithmic outputs that can be explored interactively. Each run with a seed produces reproducible results you can modify and export.
